summary: cautilus init installs the CLI plus Cautilus Agent into a host repo
anchor: install CLI + Cautilus Agent into a host repo

summary: cautilus update refreshes the CLI and the checked-in Cautilus Agent in a repo
anchor: refresh the CLI and the checked-in Cautilus Agent

summary: cautilus version --verbose inspects local version provenance
anchor: inspect local version provenance

summary: cautilus version --verbose is the quickest binary-only summary of the product surface
anchor: quickest binary-only summary of what

summary: Legacy Homebrew installs are not a supported update channel
anchor: Legacy Homebrew installs are not a supported update channel anymore

summary: cautilus init adapter generates a minimal repo-local adapter scaffold
anchor: generate a minimal repo-local adapter scaffold

summary: cautilus doctor adapter resolves and validates the adapter
anchor: resolve and validate the adapter

summary: Each starter ships a pre-filled adapter, canonical input fixture, and a README
anchor: ships a pre-filled `cautilus-adapter.yaml`, a canonical input fixture

summary: A minimal adapter must wire at least one real runnable path to reach doctor ready
anchor: must wire at least one real runnable path to reach `doctor ready`

summary: --codex-home-mode isolated keeps user config and session state out of the eval
anchor: keeps user config and session state out of the eval

summary: --codex-auth-mode inherit copies only Codex auth into the isolated home
anchor: copies only Codex auth into the isolated home

summary: --codex-auth-mode env relies on OPENAI_API_KEY for auth
anchor: intentionally relies on `OPENAI_API_KEY`

summary: Runner reports runner_auth_missing before spending the live run when no auth source is available
anchor: reports `runner_auth_missing` before spending the whole live run

summary: evaluate fixture chooses the runner whose surfaces contains the fixture surface key
anchor: chooses the runner whose `surfaces` contains the fixture surface key

summary: The {runner_id} placeholder is available to command templates
anchor: The `{runner_id}` placeholder is available to command templates

summary: Fixture runtime reports observed proof class fixture-smoke even when adapter declares a stronger runner class
anchor: the observed proof class is `fixture-smoke`

summary: dev/repo and dev/skill non-fixture runtime may report coding-agent-messaging class only with matching runtime telemetry
anchor: may report the adapter-declared `coding-agent-messaging` class

summary: cautilus doctor binary reports binary health with no repo required
anchor: binary health, no repo required

summary: cautilus doctor commands performs command discovery safe for wrapper tooling and agent runtimes
anchor: command discovery, safe for wrapper tooling and agent runtimes

summary: cautilus doctor status gives no-input agent orientation over binary, adapter, agent-surface, and claim state
anchor: no-input agent orientation over binary, adapter, agent-surface, and claim state

summary: cautilus doctor reports repo readiness for evaluation
anchor: repo readiness for evaluation

summary: doctor --next-action gives one current onboarding step plus the exact continuation loop
anchor: one current onboarding step plus the exact continuation loop

summary: doctor --scope agent-surface checks local agent-skill discoverability in a consumer repo
anchor: local agent-skill discoverability in a consumer repo

summary: cautilus discover claims performs declared-claim discovery and proof planning
anchor: declared-claim discovery and proof planning

summary: cautilus discover scenarios emits the scenario-normalization catalog for proposal-input examples
anchor: scenario-normalization catalog, for agents that need proposal-input examples

summary: cautilus <subcommand> --help exits 0 for the registered native command surface including grouped topics
anchor: exits `0` for the registered native command surface

summary: doctor status --json emits cautilus.agent_status.v1, a read-only orientation packet
anchor: It emits `cautilus.agent_status.v1`: a read-only orientation packet

summary: When runner proof is not ready, next action can point at runner assessment setup before the first eval loop
anchor: that next action can point at runner assessment setup

summary: doctor --scope agent-surface verifies only the Cautilus Agent and local agent-surface install
anchor: verify only the Cautilus Agent and local agent-surface install

summary: default doctor (--scope repo) verifies the repo has a real runnable evaluation path
anchor: verify the repo has a real runnable evaluation path

summary: Each doctor check separates stable contract from run-specific observation
anchor: separates stable contract from run-specific observation

summary: Each doctor check reports id, ok, meaning, and detail fields
anchor: `id` names the machine-readable readiness condition, `ok` reports the boolean

summary: External prerequisite checks put resolved binary path in detail when present and searched PATH when missing
anchor: put the resolved binary path in `detail` when present

summary: Repo-scope ready doctor payload includes first_bounded_run starter packet loop plus scenario-normalization catalog
anchor: the JSON payload includes `first_bounded_run`

summary: doctor --adapter-name validates a named adapter under .agents/cautilus-adapters/ at repo scope
anchor: run `cautilus doctor --repo-root /path/to/repo --adapter-name <name>`

summary: Default discovery starts from adapter-owned claim entries or README/AGENTS/CLAUDE and follows local Markdown links to depth 3
anchor: follows repo-local Markdown links to depth 3

summary: --previous with --refresh-plan enables deterministic refresh planning inside discover
anchor: for deterministic refresh planning inside the same discover workflow

summary: The refresh-plan packet includes refreshSummary with counts, changed source hotspots, and next actions
anchor: includes `refreshSummary`, which gives agent-readable counts

summary: The CLI rejects writing refresh-plan packets over saved claim-map paths due to schema mismatch
anchor: The CLI rejects that overwrite

summary: Normal discovery output is cautilus.claim_proof_plan.v1, a proof plan rather than a verdict
anchor: it is a proof plan rather than a verdict

summary: discover claims status summarizes an existing claim packet without rescanning with bounded examples
anchor: summarize an existing claim packet without rescanning

summary: The status summary includes gitState signaling staleness that requires a refresh-plan before review or planning
anchor: The summary includes `gitState`; when it reports `isStale=true`

summary: The status summary includes evidenceSatisfaction exposing satisfied claims and evidence refs
anchor: includes `evidenceSatisfaction` so satisfied claims and their evidence refs are visible

summary: gitState.isStale tracks recorded claim-source changes, not every HEAD change
anchor: tracks recorded claim-source changes, not every HEAD change

summary: Commits adding only generated claim artifacts report headDrift with fresh-with-head-drift, allowing review to continue
anchor: `gitState` reports `headDrift=true` with `comparisonStatus=fresh-with-head-drift`

summary: discover claims extraction-input emits the deterministic agent-extraction input packet with hashes, template, hints, and bounds
anchor: emit the deterministic agent-extraction input packet

summary: discover claims apply-extraction validates an agent extraction result, anchors excerpts, and composes the agent proof plan
anchor: validate an agent extraction result, anchor every verbatim excerpt

summary: Neither extraction command calls an LLM; invalid claims are rejected into rejectedClaims rather than silently dropped
anchor: Neither command calls an LLM; unanchored, unbounded, or out-of-scope claims are rejected

summary: discover claims review-input prepares bounded deterministic review clusters for an agent or subagent
anchor: prepare bounded deterministic review clusters for an agent or subagent

summary: review-input emits cautilus.claim_review_input.v1 and does not call an LLM or mark claims satisfied
anchor: does not call an LLM or mark claims satisfied

summary: review input may include possibleEvidenceRefs as reviewer hints when adapter evidence_roots have bundles
anchor: review input may include `possibleEvidenceRefs`; these are reviewer hints only

summary: Already-satisfied or already-reviewed claims are skipped by default and recorded under skippedClaims
anchor: already reviewed are skipped by default and recorded under `skippedClaims`

summary: discover claims apply-review merges reviewed labels and evidence refs back into a claim packet
anchor: merge reviewed labels and evidence refs back into a claim packet

summary: apply-review rejects evidenceStatus=satisfied unless a direct or verified evidence ref supports the claim
anchor: rejects `evidenceStatus=satisfied` unless a direct or verified evidence ref

summary: discover claims validate emits cautilus.claim_validation_report.v1 and fails fast on invalid packet shape or evidence refs
anchor: fail fast on invalid packet shape or evidence refs

summary: discover claims validate does not mutate the packet or search for evidence
anchor: It does not mutate the packet or search for evidence

summary: evaluate claims plan turns ready cautilus-eval claims into cautilus.claim_eval_plan.v1
anchor: turn ready `cautilus-eval` claims into `cautilus.claim_eval_plan.v1`

summary: Claims marked evidenceStatus=satisfied are skipped by default in eval planning
anchor: are skipped by default because they do not need a new eval fixture plan

summary: The eval plan packet preserves claim ids, surfaces, source refs, intents, and questions but writes no host fixtures or runners
anchor: deliberately does not write host-owned fixtures or runners

summary: review-input, apply-review, and evaluate claims plan reject stale claim packets by default unless --allow-stale-claims overrides
anchor: reject stale claim packets by default; `--allow-stale-claims` is an explicit operator override

summary: cautilus discover live-targets lists live consumer targets the host repo exposes to Cautilus
anchor: list live consumer targets that this host repo exposes to Cautilus

summary: cautilus evaluate live runs one bounded live scenario request against one selected instance
anchor: run one bounded live scenario request against one selected instance

summary: evaluate live prepare-request-batch turns draft scenarios into a canonical live-run request batch
anchor: turn draft scenarios into a canonical live-run request batch

summary: evaluate live scenarios runs many explicit live scenario requests against one selected instance
anchor: run many explicit live scenario requests against one selected instance

summary: discover live-targets resolves explicit instances or a probe command into one workbench_instance_catalog.v1 packet
anchor: into the same `cautilus.workbench_instance_catalog.v1` packet

summary: evaluate live takes one instance id plus one request packet and returns one bounded result packet
anchor: returns one bounded result packet

summary: prepare-request-batch turns draft scenarios or catalog-candidate packet into a canonical invocation request batch artifact
anchor: into a canonical `cautilus.live_run_invocation_request_batch.v1` artifact

summary: evaluate live scenarios schedules requests in-process, retries only on transient class when asked, and writes one aggregated batch result
anchor: schedules the requests in-process, retries only when the batch packet asks

summary: The product owns the packet boundary and status semantics
anchor: The product owns the packet boundary and status semantics

summary: The consumer owns actual launch, auth, and runtime wiring through its adapter command
anchor: consumer still owns actual launch, auth, and runtime wiring

summary: consumer_single_turn_command_template lets the same command own a product-managed multi-turn chatbot loop
anchor: own a product-managed multi-turn chatbot loop above a consumer-owned single-turn seam

summary: The loop allocates one stable per-request workspace dir and exposes it via {workspace_dir} placeholder
anchor: allocates one stable per-request workspace directory

summary: When workspace_prepare_command_template is declared, Cautilus runs that prepare command once before the first turn
anchor: runs that consumer-owned prepare command once before the first turn

summary: For persona_prompt simulator kind, the adapter provides simulator_persona_command_template calling evaluate live persona
anchor: the adapter additionally provides `simulator_persona_command_template`

summary: When consumer_evaluator_command_template is declared, Cautilus materializes evaluator input and expects an evaluator result verdict
anchor: materializes one `cautilus.live_run_evaluator_input.v1` packet and expects one

summary: The batch flow keeps prep and execution separate: prep owns request synthesis, run-scenarios owns concurrency, retry, and artifact layout
anchor: keeps prep and execution separate on purpose

summary: cautilus init run starts a fresh per-run subdirectory under one stable artifact root and pins it
anchor: start a fresh per-run subdirectory under one stable artifact root and pin it

summary: evaluate comparison prepare prepares clean baseline and candidate git worktrees for a compare run
anchor: prepare clean baseline and candidate git worktrees for a compare run

summary: cautilus doctor artifacts prune prunes older Cautilus artifact bundles
anchor: prune older Cautilus artifact bundles

summary: workspace start defaults --root to ./.cautilus/runs/ auto-created on first use and prints CAUTILUS_RUN_DIR export
anchor: defaults `--root` to `./.cautilus/runs/` (auto-created on first use)

summary: Passing --json instead of eval returns the machine-readable payload
anchor: Pass `--json` instead of `eval` when a script needs the machine-readable payload

summary: Downstream commands reuse the pinned CAUTILUS_RUN_DIR or auto-materialize a fresh runDir and print Active run to stderr
anchor: reuse that active `runDir`; if nothing is pinned they auto-materialize

summary: discover scenarios normalize chatbot normalizes chatbot-style conversation summaries
anchor: normalize chatbot-style conversation summaries

summary: discover scenarios normalize skill normalizes skill-evaluation summaries
anchor: normalize skill-evaluation summaries

summary: discover scenarios normalize workflow normalizes durable-workflow run summaries
anchor: normalize durable-workflow run summaries

summary: discover scenarios prepare-input assembles a proposal-input packet from split normalized source files
anchor: assemble a proposal-input packet from split normalized source files

summary: discover scenarios propose generates a scenario proposal packet from normalized input
anchor: generate a scenario proposal packet from normalized input

summary: discover scenarios review-conversations builds a scenario-centric conversation review packet from normalized chatbot threads
anchor: build a scenario-centric conversation review packet from normalized chatbot threads

summary: discover scenarios render-conversation-review-html renders the conversation review packet into HTML
anchor: render the conversation review packet into HTML

summary: discover scenarios summarize-telemetry summarizes scenario-level cost, token, and duration telemetry
anchor: summarize scenario-level cost, token, and duration telemetry

summary: Every normalize command plus observation and report build accepts --example-input to print a minimal valid packet to stdout
anchor: accepts `--example-input`: it prints a minimal valid packet to stdout

summary: discover scenarios --json exposes inspect commands under normalizationFamilies[*].exampleInputCli
anchor: exposes those same inspect commands under `normalizationFamilies[*].exampleInputCli`

summary: discover scenarios propose preserves the full ranked proposals list in the canonical JSON output
anchor: preserves the full ranked `proposals` list in the canonical JSON output

summary: The propose packet also emits an attentionView, a bounded human-facing shortlist derived from the ranked set
anchor: emits an `attentionView`, which is a bounded human-facing shortlist

summary: review-conversations links normalized threads to proposals and coverage hints for behavior-eval evidence review
anchor: links normalized chatbot threads to scenario proposals and coverage hints

summary: cautilus evaluate fixture runs a checked-in fixture through an adapter-owned runner and evaluates the observed packet
anchor: runs a checked-in fixture through an adapter-owned runner

summary: evaluate fixture records a top-level proof object in eval-observed.json and eval-summary.json on runner readiness
anchor: records a top-level `proof` object in `eval-observed.json` and `eval-summary.json`

summary: When runner_readiness.runners is declared, evaluate fixture selects the runner by the fixture surface key
anchor: selects the runner by the fixture surface key before rendering the command template

summary: Fixture-runtime runs report fixture-smoke and preserve adapter-declared proof class only as declared metadata
anchor: Fixture-runtime runs are reported as `fixture-smoke`

summary: cautilus evaluate observation evaluates an already-observed packet without launching the runner again
anchor: evaluates an already-observed packet without launching the runner again

summary: cautilus evaluate skill-experiment compares one sandboxed skill-clone experiment after host-owned execution
anchor: compare one sandboxed skill-clone experiment after host-owned execution

summary: The fixture dogfood variants prove app/chat and app/prompt fixture translation, adapter runner, and evaluator packet paths
anchor: prove the `app / chat` and `app / prompt` fixture translation

summary: The Codex dogfood variants run the same checked-in fixtures through the Codex backend
anchor: run the same checked-in fixtures through the Codex backend

summary: The Claude variants run the same fixtures through the Claude backend, proving multi-provider backend support
anchor: proving that the app surface runner can use more than one provider backend

summary: Backend variants are not eval live product-runner proof and report productProofReady=false until an app-owned runner assessment exists
anchor: report `productProofReady=false` until an app-owned runner assessment exists

summary: evaluate skill-experiment emits skill_clone_experiment_report.v1 with variant_ran, delta, rubric match, coverage delta, isolation notes, and a promotion recommendation
anchor: emits `cautilus.skill_clone_experiment_report.v1` with `variant_ran`

summary: evaluate skill-experiment does not install, mutate, or execute skills; host runners provide baseline and variant outputs
anchor: does not install, mutate, or execute skills

summary: --runtime fixture gives cheap command-routing proof over fixture-backed runner results
anchor: cheap command-routing proof over fixture-backed runner results

summary: --runtime fixture is not evidence of good model-backed skill execution, only that command surface, adapter, translation, and evaluation line up
anchor: It is not evidence that a model-backed skill execution is good

summary: evaluate report build builds a machine-readable evaluation report packet from explicit mode runs
anchor: build a machine-readable evaluation report packet from explicit mode runs

summary: evaluate review prepare-input assembles a durable review packet around a report
anchor: assemble a durable review packet around a report

summary: evaluate review build-prompt-input builds the product-owned meta-prompt packet
anchor: build the product-owned meta-prompt packet

summary: evaluate review render-prompt renders the review prompt
anchor: render the review prompt

summary: evaluate review variants runs every executor variant defined by an adapter
anchor: run every executor variant defined by an adapter

summary: evaluate review variants can run an ad-hoc bounded review without first building a review packet
anchor: run an ad-hoc bounded review without first building a review packet

summary: review feedback build normalizes a source-bound review outcome into review-learning evidence
anchor: normalize a source-bound review outcome into review-learning evidence

summary: review feedback summarize summarizes selected review-learning packets by method family and disposition
anchor: summarize selected review-learning packets by method family and disposition

summary: evaluate report build --example-input produces a minimal valid report packet
anchor: run `cautilus evaluate report build --example-input`

summary: evaluate review variants writes a product-owned review-summary.json plus per-variant result files
anchor: writes a product-owned `review-summary.json` (`cautilus.review_summary.v1`) plus one per-variant

summary: A variant can finish passed, blocked, or failed; blocked runs carry machine-readable reason codes
anchor: blocked runs carry machine-readable reason codes instead of prose-only abort text

summary: Local executor readiness failures such as missing auth are classified as blocked unavailable_executor, not negative verdicts
anchor: classified as blocked `unavailable_executor` results rather than negative review verdicts

summary: When one variant passes and another does not, the summary sets partialSuccess and lifts passing outputs into successfulVariantOutputs
anchor: the summary sets `partialSuccess: true` and lifts the passing outputs

summary: When --output-under-test is present, the review prompt switches into output_under_test mode treating the artifact as primary evidence
anchor: switches into `output_under_test` mode and treats the referenced artifact as primary evidence

summary: When --output-text-key is present, Cautilus extracts that JSON narrative span into the rendered prompt
anchor: extracts that JSON narrative span into the rendered prompt

summary: review feedback build writes cautilus.review_feedback.v1
anchor: `review feedback build` writes `cautilus.review_feedback.v1`

summary: review feedback build preserves source review reference plus normalized method, proposal, disposition, and note fields
anchor: preserves a source review reference plus normalized method, proposal, disposition, and note fields

summary: missing_critical disposition may omit proposal evidence, recording that the method missed a needed proposal
anchor: `missing_critical` may omit proposal evidence

summary: review feedback summarize writes cautilus.review_feedback_summary.v1
anchor: `review feedback summarize` writes `cautilus.review_feedback_summary.v1`

summary: review feedback summarize counts review-useful dispositions overall and by method family, keeping source-review refs per input
anchor: counts review-useful dispositions overall and by method family

summary: review feedback summarize does not discover active-run defaults or treat dispositions as pass/fail status
anchor: does not discover active-run defaults or treat dispositions as evaluator pass/fail

summary: evaluate evidence prepare-input prepares normalized evidence input
anchor: prepare normalized evidence input

summary: evaluate evidence bundle builds the evidence bundle
anchor: build the evidence bundle

summary: cautilus improve is the improvement front door
anchor: `cautilus improve` is the improvement front door

summary: Reports whose productRunnerProofReadiness is blocked cannot become improve inputs until runner-backed product proof is ready
anchor: blocked cannot become improve inputs until runner-backed product proof is ready

summary: improve prepare-input prepares the bounded one-shot improver input from report, review summary, and history
anchor: The bounded one-shot improver

summary: improve propose produces a bounded one-shot improvement proposal from the improve input
anchor: cautilus improve propose

summary: doctor artifacts render-self-dogfood-html refreshes the static HTML view of the current self-dogfood bundle
anchor: refresh the static HTML view of the current checked-in self-dogfood bundle

summary: doctor artifacts render-self-dogfood-experiments-html refreshes the static HTML comparison view of the latest experiments bundle
anchor: refresh the static HTML comparison view of the current latest experiments bundle

summary: The rendered HTML is read-only over summary.json, report.json, and review-summary.json, which remain source of truth
anchor: The rendered HTML is read-only over `summary.json`, `report.json`, and `review-summary.json`

summary: claude plugins validate validates the checked-in Claude marketplace and plugin manifest
anchor: validate the checked-in Claude marketplace and plugin manifest

summary: check-codex-marketplace.mjs verifies Codex can discover the repo-local marketplace entry
anchor: verify Codex can discover the repo-local marketplace entry

summary: Most underlying Node scripts are still runnable directly when a wrapper needs them without the cautilus binary
anchor: Most underlying Node scripts are still runnable directly

summary: discover live-targets and evaluate live are Go-owned and should be called through the CLI not direct node paths
anchor: are Go-owned now and should be called through the CLI
